agenda_pressure: Favor residual metatheorems that strengthen finite recognition and decidability relevance.
Prefer canonical automata and exact witness interfaces over new local proof predicates.
Use fragment work mainly when it yields intrinsic residual or candidate-tree witnesses with later automata value.
Let verified support, occurrence, and counting counterexamples steer effort away from orientation-blind support claims.
Treat subformula control as important when it feeds finite residual reachability rather than only cosmetic derivation bookkeeping.
current_bottlenecks: No unconditional subformula theorem for residualAtomicState outputs.
No proof that every accepting residual-run state stays inside one finite subformula closure.
Residual recognition still relies on external graph acceptance rather than a closed internal automaton witness.
Fragment calculi still lack intrinsic residual-state or candidate-tree interfaces.
The exact same-handed residual threshold separating occurrence from acceptance is still unclear.
desired_summary_changes: ResidualAtomicState outputs are uniformly subformula-bounded by the original sequent.
Accepting residual runs stay inside a finite subformula-bounded region with a fixed target atom.
Residual recognition is reformulated as a canonical closed branching automaton on atomic states.
One-sided fragment results gain intrinsic residual or candidate-tree witnesses instead of only translation theorems.
Same-handed residual occurrence versus acceptance is replaced by a sharp threshold or criterion.
important_verified_counterexamples: Verified counterexample to: Prove a one-sided exactness theorem for ordinary support closure: if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is left-only, then `SupportClosure Γ A ↔ HandedSupportClosure Γ A`; dually, if every formula in `Γ` and `A` is ...
Verified counterexample to: Prove exact completeness of the support closure by showing `∀ (Γ : List Tp) (A : Tp), SupportClosure Γ A ↔ (Γ ⇒ A)`.
Verified counterexample to: ∀ {Γ : List Tp} {A : Tp} {t : String}, (Γ ⇒ A) → occurs_atom t A → ∃ B ∈ Γ, occurs_atom t B
missing_bridges: From residual normal forms to unconditional root subformula control.
From root subformula control to finite reachable-region control along accepting residual runs.
From residual-graph acceptance to a self-contained branching automaton.
From translation-level one-sided conservativity to intrinsic fragment residual or candidate-tree witnesses.
From same-handed support counterexamples to exact residual-state acceptance criteria.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize residual root and run subformula invariants, then use that control to internalize residual recognition as a closed branching automaton; treat fragment work as secondary unless it yields intrinsic residual witnesses.
next_direction.label: subformula_bounded_residual_automata
next_direction.rationale: Residual-state and residual-graph exactness are already in place, while the verified failures of ordinary support exactness, atom-occurrence containment, and counting-style bounds point the frontier toward finite residual control rather than further support-level refinements.

overexplored_patterns: Singleton or atom-only normal forms already dominated by arbitrary-context exact theorems.
Proof predicates or witness refactorings that only repackage an existing iff characterization.
Fragment corollaries weaker than proved sequent-level conservativity.
Ordinary support, atom-occurrence, or counting claims outside exact handed or residual interfaces.
Graph or tree encodings that duplicate existing recognition results without adding finite residual control.
theory_snapshot: The central structure now gives exact arbitrary-context derivability through aligned CandidateTree, HandedSupportClosure, residualAtomicState, and residual-graph acceptance interfaces, with full one-sided conservativity already established at the sequent level. The main frontier is no longer extensional completeness but finite residual control: the theory still lacks an unconditional subformula bound for residual roots, a proof that accepting residual runs stay inside a fixed subformula-bounded region, and a closed internal branching automaton replacing external graph acceptance. Verified counterexamples to ordinary SupportClosure exactness, flat atom-occurrence containment, counting bounds, and same-handed ordinary-support exactness show that durable progress must come from handed or residual criteria rather than orientation-blind support claims.
